question_text: A student has a mass of 50 kg on Earth where g = 10 N/kg. On the Moon, the gravitational field strength is 1.6 N/kg. Calculate the student's weight on the Moon and explain why it is different from their weight on Earth.
correct_answer: Weight on Moon = 50 × 1.6 = 80 N. The weight is different because the Moon has a weaker gravitational field strength than Earth (1.6 N/kg compared to 10 N/kg), so it pulls less strongly on the student's mass.
explanation: Weight depends on both mass and gravitational field strength using W = mg. Although the student's mass stays the same everywhere, the gravitational pull is weaker on the Moon, making their weight much smaller.
